seoTitle: How to Add Videos to Zendesk Help Center Articles
metaDescription: Embed video into your Zendesk Guide articles using the native toolbar for standard links, or the code editor for platforms that need a custom iframe.
intro: Long blocks of text can only explain so much, and embedding video into your Help Center articles gives customers a faster way to follow along. This guide covers the native embed option in the article editor as well as the code editor workaround for video sources that need a custom iframe.

steps.heading: Copy your video URL
steps.body: Grab the shareable link to your video from platforms like YouTube, Vimeo, or Wistia.

steps.heading: Open the article editor
steps.body: Go into your active Guide article draft and place your cursor where you want the video to appear.

steps.heading: Use the embed option
steps.body: Click the article components dropdown and choose the embed option, then paste your URL and confirm the preview card looks correct.

steps.heading: Enable unsafe content if needed
steps.body: For video sources the native embed doesn't support, adjust your security settings to allow unsafe content for custom code.

steps.heading: Add a custom iframe
steps.body: Switch the article into source code mode and insert your HTML iframe code for the video.

steps.heading: Test in the live view
steps.body: Save your draft and check the live Help Center preview to confirm the video loads and displays properly.

faq.question: Do all video platforms work with the native embed tool?
faq.answer: Most major platforms work with a simple link paste, but some formats may require the custom HTML iframe approach instead.

faq.question: Is it safe to enable the unsafe content setting?
faq.answer: Enabling it lets you use trusted custom code like an iframe, but you should only paste code from sources you trust.

faq.question: Will embedded videos resize for mobile viewers?
faq.answer: You may need to add custom CSS class wraps to prevent the video from cropping or overflowing on mobile devices.

faq.question: Can I embed a video that's hosted internally rather than on YouTube?
faq.answer: Yes, as long as you have a shareable or embeddable link, the same embed or iframe approach should work.
